summary: Do not ship D1 as-is: the write path can escape `ROOT` through symlinks, and markdown preview executes attacker-controlled HTML/JS in the app origin.

findings.title: Write confinement is bypassable through symlinked targets because `mustExist=false` only verifies the parent path when it already fully exists
findings.recommendation: For writes, resolve and validate every existing path segment, then refuse if any segment is a symlink out of `ROOT`. Also open the destination without following symlinks (`O_NOFOLLOW`/lstat+rename pattern) so an existing symlink file cannot be overwritten outside the root. The current `realpath(target)` early return on ENOENT plus `mkdir(..., {recursive:true})` / `writeFile(abs, ...)` at lines 118-119 allows escapes such as `ROOT/symlink/new.txt` when `symlink -> /outside`, or writing through an existing symlink file inside an in-root parent.

findings.title: Markdown preview is XSS: `marked` output is injected with `innerHTML` after only stripping `<script>` tags
findings.recommendation: Sanitize rendered markdown with a real HTML sanitizer or disable raw HTML entirely before assigning to `innerHTML`. As written, payloads like event-handler attributes, `javascript:` URLs, SVG/MathML, or `iframe/srcdoc` survive the regex and execute in the file-browser origin, which then lets malicious `.md` content drive `/api/tree` and `/api/file` with same-origin privileges.
